60	    func applicationDidFinishLaunching(_ notification: Notification) {
61	        NSApplication.shared.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
62	
63	        // No .app bundle ships with this SwiftPM executable, so AppKit has no
64	        // static icon slot — set the Dock / Cmd+Tab icon programmatically.
65	        if let icon = AppIconRenderer.makeImage(pixelSize: 1024) {
66	            NSApplication.shared.applicationIconImage = icon
67	        }
68	
69	        let guard_ = AppPidGuard(cacheDir: Loggers.cacheDir)
70	        do {
71	            try guard_.acquire()
72	        } catch AppPidGuardError.alreadyRunning(let pid, _) {
73	            let alert = NSAlert()
74	            alert.messageText = "AI Usages Tracker is already running"
75	            alert.informativeText = "Another instance is active (PID \(pid)). Use the menu bar icon to quit it before launching a new one."
76	            alert.runModal()
77	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
78	            return
79	        } catch {
80	            let alert = NSAlert()
81	            alert.messageText = "Failed to start AI Usages Tracker"
82	            alert.informativeText = error.localizedDescription
83	            alert.runModal()
84	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
85	            return
86	        }
87	        pidGuard = guard_
88	
89	        Loggers.setPreferences(Self.sharedPreferences)
90	
91	        MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.seedIfNeeded(preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
92	        ChartConfigurationsSeeder.seedIfNeeded(preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
93	        OutageWarningMigration.migrateIfNeeded(preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
94	
95	        // Reconcile launch-at-login preference with system state — the user may
96	        // have disabled the entry in System Settings > Login Items directly.
97	        let launchService = LaunchAtLoginService.shared
98	        if Self.sharedPreferences.launchAtLogin != launchService.isEnabled {
99	            Self.sharedPreferences.launchAtLogin = launchService.isEnabled
100	        }

102	        let fileManager = UsagesFileManager.shared
103	        let refreshState = RefreshState()
104	        let claudeConnector = ClaudeCodeConnector()
105	        let claudeStatus = ClaudeStatusConnector()
106	        let codexConnector = CodexConnector()
107	        let codexStatus = CodexStatusConnector()
108	        let copilotConnector = CopilotConnector()
109	        let poller = UsagePoller(
110	            connectors: [claudeConnector, codexConnector, copilotConnector],
111	            statusConnectors: [claudeStatus, codexStatus],
112	            fileManager: fileManager,
113	            refreshState: refreshState,
114	            preferences: Self.sharedPreferences
115	        )
116	        let claudeMonitor = ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor(
117	            fileManager: fileManager,
118	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak poller] _ in
119	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
120	            }
121	        )
122	        let codexMonitor = CodexActiveAccountMonitor(
123	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak codexConnector, weak poller] _ in
124	                await codexConnector?.invalidateEmailCache()
125	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
126	            }
127	        )
128	        let copilotMonitor = CopilotActiveAccountMonitor(
129	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak copilotConnector, weak poller] _ in
130	                await copilotConnector?.invalidateLoginCache()
131	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
132	            }
133	        )
134	        VendorRegistry.resetForTesting()
135	        ClaudeCodePlugin.register(connector: claudeConnector, status: claudeStatus, monitor: claudeMonitor)
136	        CodexPlugin.register(connector: codexConnector, status: codexStatus, monitor: codexMonitor)
137	        CopilotCLIPlugin.register(connector: copilotConnector, monitor: copilotMonitor)
138	        let snapshotRecorder = SnapshotRecorder()
139	        let historyReader = UsageHistoryReader(rootPath: snapshotRecorder.rootPath)
140	        let snapshotScheduler = SnapshotScheduler(
141	            fileManager: fileManager,
142	            recorder: snapshotRecorder
143	        )
144	        self.poller = poller
145	        self.snapshotScheduler = snapshotScheduler
146	        self.refreshState = refreshState
147	
148	        let usagesPath = fileManager.filePath
149	        let fileWatcher = UsagesFileWatcher(path: usagesPath)
150	        let store = UsageStore(fileWatcher: fileWatcher, preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
151	        self.usageStore = store
152	        Self.sharedStore = store
153	
154	        Self.sharedUpdateState.dismissedVersions = Set(Self.sharedPreferences.updatesDismissedVersions)
155	
156	        setupStatusItem(store: store, refreshState: refreshState, historyReader: historyReader)
157	        trackStoreChanges(store: store)
158	        setupUpdateScheduler()
159	
160	        Task {
161	            await StartupMigrationRunner(fileManager: fileManager).run()
162	            store.start()
163	            await poller.start()
164	            await snapshotScheduler.start()
165	            for bundle in VendorRegistry.bundles {
166	                await bundle.activeAccountMonitor?.start()
167	            }
168	            if let scheduler = self.updateScheduler {
169	                await scheduler.start()
170	            }
171	        }
172	    }
173	
174	    private func setupUpdateScheduler() {
175	        // In a development build (swift run) there's no Info.plist version, so
176	        // we fall back to 0.0.0 — every release will then be reported as newer,
177	        // but the existing "Skip this version" flow ensures the alert isn't
178	        // re-shown until a different version ships.
179	        let effectiveVersion = Self.currentAppVersion() ?? AppVersion(string: "0.0.0")!
180	        let bundlePath = Bundle.main.bundleURL.path
181	        let detector = InstallationDetector(bundlePath: bundlePath)
182	        self.installationDetector = detector
183	        self.updateInstaller = UpdateInstaller()
184	        self.updateDownloader = UpdateDownloader()
185	        self.brewUpgradeRunner = BrewUpgradeRunner()
186	        let scheduler = UpdateScheduler(
187	            checker: UpdateChecker(),
188	            detector: detector,
189	            currentVersion: effectiveVersion,
190	            preferencesAccessor: { Self.sharedPreferences },
191	            stateAccessor: { Self.sharedUpdateState },
192	            onUpdateAvailable: { [weak self] update, kind in
193	                self?.presentUpdateAlert(update: update, kind: kind)
194	            }
195	        )
196	        self.updateScheduler = scheduler
197	        Self.sharedUpdateScheduler = scheduler
198	        Self.sharedTriggerUpdateInstall = { [weak self] in
199	            self?.triggerUpdateInstall()
200	        }
201	        Self.sharedTriggerRestart = { [weak self] in
202	            self?.triggerRestart()
203	        }
204	    }
205	
206	    static func currentAppVersion() -> AppVersion? {
207	        guard let raw = Bundle.main.object(forInfoDictionaryKey: "CFBundleShortVersionString") as? String else {
208	            return nil
209	        }
210	        return AppVersion(string: raw)
211	    }
212	
213	    /// Human-readable label for the running app version, falling back to a
214	    /// "development build" marker when launched via `swift run` (no Info.plist).

217	    }

225	    private func setupStatusItem(
226	        store: UsageStore,
227	        refreshState: RefreshState,
228	        historyReader: UsageHistoryReader
229	    ) {
230	        let item = NSStatusBar.system.statusItem(withLength: NSStatusItem.variableLength)
231	        statusItem = item

233	        let popover = NSPopover()
234	        popover.behavior = .transient
235	        let hosting = NSHostingController(

238	                refreshState: refreshState,
239	                historyReader: historyReader,
240	                updateState: Self.sharedUpdateState,
241	                onRefresh: { [weak self] in
242	                    await self?.poller?.pollOnce(now: Date(), force: true)
243	                },
244	                onOpenSettings: { [weak self] in
245	                    self?.openSettings()
246	                },
247	                onQuit: { [weak self] in
248	                    self?.quit()
249	                },
250	                onInstallUpdate: { [weak self] in
251	                    self?.triggerUpdateInstall()
252	                },
253	                onRestartUpdate: { [weak self] in
254	                    self?.triggerRestart()
255	                },
256	                onSkipUpdate: { [weak self] in
257	                    self?.skipCurrentUpdate()
258	                },
259	                onLaterUpdate: { [weak self] in
260	                    self?.laterUpdate()
261	                }
262	            )
263	        )
264	        // Let SwiftUI's intrinsic size drive the popover so it grows with content
265	        // up to the screen-ratio cap enforced inside UsageDetailsView.
266	        hosting.sizingOptions = [.preferredContentSize]
267	        popover.contentViewController = hosting
268	        self.popover = popover
269	
270	        menuBarRefreshController = MenuBarRefreshController { [weak self] key in
271	            guard let self, let button = self.statusItem?.button else { return }
272	            button.image = MenuBarLabelRenderer.render(
273	                segments: key.segments,
274	                separator: key.separator,
275	                fallbackText: key.text,
276	                isDarkMenuBar: key.isDark,
277	                isUnconfigured: key.isUnconfigured
278	            )
279	        }
280	
281	        if let button = item.button {
282	            button.target = self
283	            button.action = #selector(togglePopover(_:))
284	            // The menu bar appearance can change (wallpaper-driven tinting) without
285	            // the app's effectiveAppearance changing, so we observe the button itself.
286	            appearanceObserver = button.observe(\.effectiveAppearance, options: [.new]) { [weak self] _, _ in
287	                Task { @MainActor in
288	                    guard let self, let button = self.statusItem?.button else { return }
289	                    let isDark = button.effectiveAppearance.bestMatch(from: [.darkAqua, .aqua]) == .darkAqua
290	                    guard self.menuBarRefreshController?.shouldHandleAppearanceChange(isDark: isDark) == true else { return }
291	                    self.refreshStatusItemImage()
292	                }
293	            }

300	    private func startRenderCountExportIfRequested() {
301	        guard let exportPath = ProcessInfo.processInfo.environment["AI_TRACKER_EXPORT_RENDER_COUNT"],
302	              !exportPath.isEmpty,
303	              renderCountExportTask == nil else { return }
304	        let url = URL(fileURLWithPath: exportPath)
305	        Loggers.app.log(.info, "Exporting menu bar render count to \(exportPath) every 2 s")
306	        renderCountExportTask = Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
307	            while !Task.isCancelled {
308	                try? await Task.sleep(nanoseconds: 2 * 1_000_000_000)
309	                guard !Task.isCancelled else { break }
310	                let count = self?.menuBarRefreshController?.renderCount ?? 0
311	                let payload = Data("\(count)\n".utf8)
312	                do {
313	                    try payload.write(to: url, options: .atomic)
314	                } catch {
315	                    Loggers.app.log(.warning, "render count export failed: \(error)")
316	                }
317	            }
318	        }
319	    }

321	    private func refreshStatusItemImage() {
322	        guard let store = usageStore,
323	              let button = statusItem?.button,
324	              let controller = menuBarRefreshController else { return }
325	        let isDark = button.effectiveAppearance.bestMatch(from: [.darkAqua, .aqua]) == .darkAqua
326	        controller.refresh(key: MenuBarRenderKey(
327	            segments: store.menuBarSegments,
328	            text: store.menuBarText,
329	            separator: Self.sharedPreferences.menuBarSeparator,
330	            isDark: isDark,
331	            isUnconfigured: Self.sharedPreferences.menuBarSegments.isEmpty
332	        ))
333	    }
334	
335	    @objc private func togglePopover(_ sender: Any?) {
336	        guard let popover, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
337	        if popover.isShown {
338	            popover.performClose(sender)
339	        } else {
340	            popover.show(relativeTo: button.bounds, of: button, preferredEdge: .minY)
341	            popover.contentViewController?.view.window?.makeKey()
342	        }
343	    }
344	
345	    // MARK: - Observation
346	
347	    /// Re-arms `withObservationTracking` after every change so the status item
348	    /// keeps mirroring the store. Tracking fires exactly once per registration.
349	    private func trackStoreChanges(store: UsageStore) {
350	        withObservationTracking {
351	            _ = store.menuBarSegments
352	            _ = store.menuBarText
353	        } onChange: { [weak self] in
354	            Task { @MainActor in
355	                guard let self, let store = self.usageStore else { return }
356	                self.refreshStatusItemImage()
357	                self.trackStoreChanges(store: store)
358	            }
359	        }
360	    }
361	
362	    // MARK: - Settings
363	
364	    private func openSettings() {
365	        popover?.performClose(nil)
366	        // .accessory apps have no dock presence; showSettingsWindow: silently fails
367	        // unless we temporarily switch to .regular so AppKit can make the window key.
368	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
369	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
370	
371	        // SwiftUI creates the Settings window lazily on first showSettingsWindow: —
372	        // if it already exists, reuse it directly to avoid responder-chain races
373	        // that occur during the popover-dismissal + activation-policy transition.
374	        if let settingsWindow = Self.findSettingsWindow() {
375	            settingsWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
376	        } else {
377	            // First-time path: AppKit needs a runloop tick after the .accessory →
378	            // .regular transition to install the standard App menu (which is where
379	            // SwiftUI wires the Settings… action). sendAction with a nil target
380	            // relies on that wiring, so we trigger the menu item directly once
381	            // AppKit has had a chance to build it.
382	            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.15) {
383	                Self.triggerSettingsMenuItem()
384	                if let settingsWindow = Self.findSettingsWindow() {
385	                    settingsWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
386	                }
387	            }
388	        }
389	
390	        // Restore .accessory policy once the settings window is closed.
391	        if settingsWindowObserver == nil {
392	            // Use selector-based observer so the callback runs on @MainActor naturally,
393	            // avoiding Sendable-crossing issues with block-based addObserver(queue:).
394	            settingsWindowObserver = self
395	            NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
396	                self,
397	                selector: #selector(handleSettingsWindowWillClose(_:)),
398	                name: NSWindow.willCloseNotification,
399	                object: nil
400	            )
401	        }
402	    }
403	
404	    @objc private func handleSettingsWindowWillClose(_ notification: Notification) {
405	        // Only react to the Settings window closing — popovers and other
406	        // transient windows also emit willClose.
407	        guard let closingWindow = notification.object as? NSWindow else { return }
408	        let windowIdentifier = closingWindow.identifier?.rawValue ?? ""
409	        guard windowIdentifier.contains("Settings") || windowIdentifier.contains("Preferences") else { return }
410	        // willClose fires while the window is still visible and before
411	        // AppKit tears it down; defer to the next runloop tick so the
412	        // activation-policy change isn't overridden by pending AppKit work.
413	        DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
414	            NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
415	            // Accessory transition doesn't always remove the app from the
416	            // Cmd+Tab / app-switcher list until another app is activated.
417	            if let frontmost = NSWorkspace.shared.runningApplications.first(where: {
418	                $0.isActive && $0.processIdentifier != ProcessInfo.processInfo.processIdentifier
419	            }) {
420	                frontmost.activate()
421	            } else {
422	                NSApp.hide(nil)
423	            }
424	            guard let self else { return }
425	            NotificationCenter.default.removeObserver(self, name: NSWindow.willCloseNotification, object: nil)
426	            self.settingsWindowObserver = nil
427	        }
428	    }
429	
430	    /// SwiftUI tags its Settings window with an identifier containing "Settings"
431	    /// (or "Preferences" on older SDKs). Match on the stable identifier rather
432	    /// than localized titles.
433	    private static func findSettingsWindow() -> NSWindow? {
434	        NSApp.windows.first { window in
435	            let identifier = window.identifier?.rawValue ?? ""
436	            return identifier.contains("Settings") || identifier.contains("Preferences")
437	        }
438	    }
439	
440	    /// Walk the main menu looking for the App menu's Settings… (or Preferences…)
441	    /// item and invoke it. Going through the menu guarantees the action is
442	    /// routed to whichever responder SwiftUI wired up, instead of relying on
443	    /// `sendAction(_:to:from:)` to resolve a nil target in the responder chain
444	    /// — which is unreliable right after an activation-policy switch.
445	    private static func triggerSettingsMenuItem() {
446	        guard let mainMenu = NSApp.mainMenu else { return }
447	        for topItem in mainMenu.items {
448	            guard let submenu = topItem.submenu else { continue }
449	            for (index, item) in submenu.items.enumerated() {
450	                let title = item.title
451	                if title.hasPrefix("Settings") || title.hasPrefix("Preferences") {
452	                    submenu.performActionForItem(at: index)
453	                    return
454	                }
455	            }
456	        }
457	    }
458	
459	    // MARK: - Updates
460	
461	    private func presentUpdateAlert(update: AvailableUpdate, kind: InstallationKind) {
462	        let alert = NSAlert()
463	        alert.icon = NSApplication.shared.applicationIconImage
464	        alert.messageText = "Update available"
465	        alert.informativeText = "Version \(update.version.rawValue) of AI Usages Tracker is available. Install it now?"
466	        alert.alertStyle = .informational
467	        let installLabel = kind == .homebrewCask ? "Update via Homebrew" : "Install"
468	        alert.addButton(withTitle: installLabel)
469	        alert.addButton(withTitle: "Later")
470	        alert.addButton(withTitle: "Skip this version")
471	        // .accessory apps don't get focus by default; activate so the modal alert
472	        // surfaces above other windows when the user is mid-task.
473	        let previousPolicy = NSApp.activationPolicy()
474	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
475	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
476	        defer { NSApp.setActivationPolicy(previousPolicy) }
477	
478	        switch alert.runModal() {
479	        case .alertFirstButtonReturn:
480	            triggerUpdateInstall()
481	        case .alertSecondButtonReturn:
482	            break
483	        case .alertThirdButtonReturn:
484	            skipCurrentUpdate()
485	        default:
486	            break
487	        }
488	    }
489	
490	    /// Kicks off the in-app preparation pipeline (download/extract for manual
491	    /// installs, `brew upgrade --cask` streaming for Homebrew). The app stays
492	    /// alive throughout, with progress mirrored into `UpdateState`. When the
493	    /// pipeline reaches `readyToRestart`, the popover banner exposes a
494	    /// "Restart now" button that invokes `triggerRestart()`.
495	    private func triggerUpdateInstall() {
496	        // Re-entrancy guard: the user can hit "Install" multiple times across
497	        // the popover and Settings — only the first click should do work.
498	        if activeInstallTask != nil { return }
499	        guard let detector = installationDetector,
500	              let downloader = updateDownloader,
501	              let brewRunner = brewUpgradeRunner,
502	              let update = Self.sharedUpdateState.availableUpdate else {
503	            return
504	        }
505	        let detectedKind = Self.sharedUpdateState.installationKind ?? .manual
506	        let runningPath = Bundle.main.bundleURL.path
507	        // For manual installs, finalization needs an actual `.app` bundle path
508	        // — fine when running from /Applications/Foo.app, but `swift run` or
509	        // any ad-hoc binary lives in a flat directory. Prompt the user for an
510	        // installation directory and synthesize the target `.app` path.
511	        let bundlePath: String
512	        if detectedKind == .manual && !runningPath.hasSuffix(".app") {
513	            guard let chosen = Self.promptForInstallDirectory(startingAt: runningPath) else {
514	                return
515	            }
516	            bundlePath = (chosen as NSString).appendingPathComponent("\(Self.appBundleDisplayName).app")
517	        } else {
518	            bundlePath = runningPath
519	        }
520	
521	        Self.sharedUpdateState.setPreparing()
522	        activeInstallBundlePath = bundlePath
523	
524	        activeInstallTask = Task { [weak self] in
525	            do {
526	                // Resolve brew at install time too — the user may have just
527	                // installed Homebrew since the periodic check.
528	                let brewPath = await detector.brewExecutablePath()
529	                let effectiveKind: InstallationKind = (detectedKind == .homebrewCask && brewPath != nil) ? .homebrewCask : .manual
530	                await MainActor.run { self?.activeInstallKind = effectiveKind }
531	
532	                switch effectiveKind {
533	                case .homebrewCask:
534	                    guard let brew = brewPath else {
535	                        // Should never happen given effectiveKind logic above,
536	                        // but bail safely rather than running brew with a nil path.
537	                        await MainActor.run { Self.sharedUpdateState.setFailed("Homebrew binary disappeared after detection.") }
538	                        return
539	                    }
540	                    try await brewRunner.runUpgrade(
541	                        brewExecutablePath: brew,
542	                        caskName: InstallationDetector.homebrewCaskName,
543	                        onEvent: { event in
544	                            Task { @MainActor in
545	                                if case .outputLine(let line) = event {
546	                                    Self.sharedUpdateState.setRunningHomebrew(lastLine: line)
547	                                }
548	                            }
549	                        }
550	                    )
551	                    // Brew has already swapped the bundle on disk — no staged path.
552	                    await MainActor.run { Self.sharedUpdateState.setReadyToRestart(stagedAppPath: nil) }
553	
554	                case .manual:
555	                    let stagedURL = try await downloader.downloadAndStage(
556	                        update: update,
557	                        onEvent: { event in
558	                            Task { @MainActor in
559	                                Self.applyDownloadEvent(event)
560	                            }
561	                        }
562	                    )
563	                    await MainActor.run { Self.sharedUpdateState.setReadyToRestart(stagedAppPath: stagedURL.path) }
564	                }
565	            } catch is CancellationError {
566	                await MainActor.run { Self.sharedUpdateState.setFailed("Install cancelled.") }
567	            } catch {
568	                let message = String(describing: error)
569	                await MainActor.run { Self.sharedUpdateState.setFailed(message) }
570	            }
571	            // Clear the re-entrancy guard synchronously before returning so a
572	            // user clicking Retry immediately after a failure isn't dropped.
573	            await MainActor.run { self?.activeInstallTask = nil }
574	        }
575	    }
576	
577	    /// Translates an `UpdateDownloadEvent` into a phase update.
578	    @MainActor
579	    private static func applyDownloadEvent(_ event: UpdateDownloadEvent) {
580	        switch event {
581	        case .progress(let received, let total):
582	            Self.sharedUpdateState.setDownloading(received: received, total: total)
583	        case .verifying:
584	            Self.sharedUpdateState.setVerifying()
585	        case .extracting:
586	            Self.sharedUpdateState.setExtracting()
587	        }
588	    }
589	
590	    /// User clicked "Restart now": build a tiny finalize script (swap-and-relaunch
591	    /// for manual, relaunch-only for brew), launch it detached, then quit.
592	    private func triggerRestart() {
593	        guard let installer = updateInstaller,
594	              let update = Self.sharedUpdateState.availableUpdate,
595	              let bundlePath = activeInstallBundlePath,
596	              let kind = activeInstallKind else {
597	            return
598	        }
599	        let stagedAppPath = Self.sharedUpdateState.stagedAppPath
600	        Self.sharedUpdateState.setRestarting()
601	        let pid = ProcessInfo.processInfo.processIdentifier
602	
603	        Task { [weak self] in
604	            do {
605	                let plan: UpdateFinalizationPlan
606	                switch kind {
607	                case .manual:
608	                    guard let staged = stagedAppPath else {
609	                        throw UpdateInstallerError.missingStagedApp(path: "(none)")
610	                    }
611	                    plan = try await installer.buildManualFinalizationPlan(
612	                        stagedAppPath: staged,
613	                        bundlePath: bundlePath,
614	                        currentPID: pid,
615	                        update: update
616	                    )
617	                case .homebrewCask:
618	                    plan = try await installer.buildHomebrewFinalizationPlan(
619	                        bundlePath: bundlePath,
620	                        currentPID: pid,
621	                        update: update
622	                    )
623	                }
624	
625	                if plan.requiresAdminPrivileges {
626	                    let confirmed = await MainActor.run {
627	                        Self.confirmAdminElevation(version: update.version.rawValue)
628	                    }
629	                    guard confirmed else {
630	                        await MainActor.run {
631	                            Self.sharedUpdateState.setReadyToRestart(stagedAppPath: stagedAppPath)
632	                        }
633	                        return
634	                    }
635	                    try await Self.launchWithAdminPrivileges(scriptPath: plan.scriptPath)
636	                } else {
637	                    try Self.launchDetached(scriptPath: plan.scriptPath)
638	                }
639	
640	                // Stop background work cleanly before terminating so the
641	                // finalize script (which polls our PID) sees a quick exit.
642	                await self?.gracefulShutdownAndTerminate()
643	            } catch {
644	                let message = String(describing: error)
645	                await MainActor.run { Self.sharedUpdateState.setFailed(message) }
646	            }
647	        }
648	    }
649	
650	    /// Display name used to synthesize the install target when running from a
651	     /// non-`.app` location (e.g. `swift run`). Mirrors `scripts/build-app-bundle.sh`.
652	    private static let appBundleDisplayName = "AI Usages Tracker"
653	
654	    /// Prompts the user for the directory in which to install the app. Returns
655	    /// nil if the user cancels. Defaults to the parent directory of the current
656	    /// binary, falling back to `/Applications` when that's a build artifact dir.
657	    @MainActor
658	    private static func promptForInstallDirectory(startingAt currentPath: String) -> String? {
659	        let panel = NSOpenPanel()
660	        panel.canChooseFiles = false
661	        panel.canChooseDirectories = true
662	        panel.canCreateDirectories = true
663	        panel.allowsMultipleSelection = false
664	        panel.title = "Select installation directory"
665	        panel.message = "Choose where AI Usages Tracker should be installed."
666	        panel.prompt = "Install here"
667	        let parent = (currentPath as NSString).deletingLastPathComponent
668	        let defaultURL: URL
669	        if parent.contains("/.build/") || parent.hasSuffix("/.build") {
670	            defaultURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/Applications")
671	        } else {
672	            defaultURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: parent)
673	        }
674	        panel.directoryURL = defaultURL
675	        let previousPolicy = NSApp.activationPolicy()
676	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
677	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
678	        defer { NSApp.setActivationPolicy(previousPolicy) }
679	        guard panel.runModal() == .OK else { return nil }
680	        return panel.url?.path
681	    }
682	
683	    @MainActor
684	    private static func confirmAdminElevation(version: String) -> Bool {
685	        let alert = NSAlert()
686	        alert.icon = NSApplication.shared.applicationIconImage
687	        alert.messageText = "Administrator permission required"
688	        alert.informativeText = "AI Usages Tracker is installed in a location that requires administrator privileges to update. macOS will prompt you for your password to install version \(version)."
689	        alert.alertStyle = .warning
690	        alert.addButton(withTitle: "Continue")
691	        alert.addButton(withTitle: "Cancel")
692	        let previousPolicy = NSApp.activationPolicy()
693	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
694	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
695	        defer { NSApp.setActivationPolicy(previousPolicy) }
696	        return alert.runModal() == .alertFirstButtonReturn
697	    }
698	
699	    /// Launch the install script as root via AppleScript's
700	    /// `do shell script ... with administrator privileges`. The bash script is
701	    /// spawned in the background (`&`) so osascript exits as soon as the user
702	    /// approves the prompt — the script itself waits for the parent app to
703	    /// terminate before performing the actual swap.
704	    private static func launchWithAdminPrivileges(scriptPath: String) async throws {
705	        let escaped = scriptPath
706	            .replacingOccurrences(of: "\\", with: "\\\\")
707	            .replacingOccurrences(of: "\"", with: "\\\"")
708	        let appleScript = """
709	        do shell script "/bin/bash \\"\(escaped)\\" >/dev/null 2>&1 &" with administrator privileges
710	        """
711	        try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation { (continuation: CheckedContinuation<Void, Error>) in
712	            DispatchQueue.global().async {
713	                let process = Process()
714	                process.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/usr/bin/osascript")
715	                process.arguments = ["-e", appleScript]
716	                process.standardOutput = FileHandle.nullDevice
717	                process.standardError = FileHandle.nullDevice
718	                do {
719	                    try process.run()
720	                } catch {
721	                    continuation.resume(throwing: error)
722	                    return
723	                }
724	                process.waitUntilExit()
725	                guard process.terminationStatus == 0 else {
726	                    continuation.resume(throwing: NSError(
727	                        domain: "AIUsagesTracker.UpdateInstaller",
728	                        code: Int(process.terminationStatus),
729	                        userInfo: [NSLocalizedDescriptionKey: "Authorization was cancelled or denied (status \(process.terminationStatus))."]
730	                    ))
731	                    return
732	                }
733	                continuation.resume()
734	            }
735	        }
736	    }
737	
738	    private func skipCurrentUpdate() {
739	        guard let version = Self.sharedUpdateState.availableUpdate?.version.rawValue else { return }
740	        Self.sharedUpdateState.dismissCurrent()
741	        var stored = Self.sharedPreferences.updatesDismissedVersions
742	        if !stored.contains(version) {
743	            stored.append(version)
744	            Self.sharedPreferences.updatesDismissedVersions = stored
745	        }
746	    }
747	
748	    private func laterUpdate() {
749	        // No-op aside from clearing the in-popover banner this session.
750	        Self.sharedUpdateState.dismissCurrent()
751	    }
752	
753	    private static func launchDetached(scriptPath: String) throws {
754	        let process = Process()
755	        process.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/bin/bash")
756	        process.arguments = [scriptPath]
757	        // Detach: redirect IO to /dev/null so the child outlives the app.
758	        process.standardOutput = FileHandle.nullDevice
759	        process.standardError = FileHandle.nullDevice
760	        process.standardInput = FileHandle.nullDevice
761	        try process.run()
762	    }
763	
764	    // MARK: - Quit
765	
766	    private func quit() {
767	        // Trigger a clean async shutdown but don't block the main thread:
768	        // NSApp.terminate is invoked from the async closure once the actors
769	        // have settled. Falls back to an immediate terminate if the task
770	        // doesn't run within a short grace window (e.g. main thread starved).
771	        Task { [weak self] in
772	            await self?.gracefulShutdownAndTerminate()
773	        }
774	    }
775	
776	    /// Awaits the actor stops, releases the PID guard, then asks AppKit to
777	    /// terminate. Used both by the menu's Quit item and by the update flow's
778	    /// "Restart now" path — in the latter case, the finalize script polls our
779	    /// PID, so a clean exit unblocks the swap quicker.
780	    @MainActor
781	    private func gracefulShutdownAndTerminate() async {
782	        let pollerRef = poller
783	        let schedulerRef = snapshotScheduler
784	        let monitors = VendorRegistry.bundles.compactMap(\.activeAccountMonitor)
785	        await pollerRef?.stop()
786	        await schedulerRef?.stop()
787	        for monitor in monitors {
788	            await monitor.stop()
789	        }
790	        usageStore?.stop()
791	        pidGuard?.release()
792	        NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
793	    }
794	}

content.thinking:
I'm reviewing the existing AppPreferences test patterns to understand the structure, then checking what loggers are available. Since the roadmap calls for shipping with an empty seed list initially, I'll keep the implementation pragmatic: store the cache in a JSON file next to the usages cache, with preferences holding the core data.

Now I need to look at FileLogger to understand how it works, and check the Outage structure to see what the affected_components field requires.

1	import Foundation
2	
3	// MARK: - Root
4	
5	/// The top-level file model: `{ usages: [...], outages?: [...] }`.
6	///
7	/// `outages` is an optional sibling written by an upstream status-fetching process.
8	/// When no incident is ongoing the upstream writer is expected to drop the key
9	/// entirely; the encoder here honours that convention by emitting nothing when
10	/// the in-memory array is empty.
11	public struct UsagesFile: Codable, Equatable, Sendable {
12	    public var usages: [VendorUsageEntry]
13	    public var outages: [Outage]
14	
15	    public init(usages: [VendorUsageEntry] = [], outages: [Outage] = []) {
16	        self.usages = usages
17	        self.outages = outages
18	    }
19	
20	    /// Grouped view for the UI — vendors without outages are omitted.
21	    public var outagesByVendor: [Vendor: [Outage]] {
22	        Dictionary(grouping: outages, by: \.vendor)
23	    }
24	
25	    private enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
26	        case usages, outages
27	    }
28	
29	    public init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
30	        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
31	        usages = try container.decodeIfPresent([VendorUsageEntry].self, forKey: .usages) ?? []
32	        outages = try container.decodeIfPresent([Outage].self, forKey: .outages) ?? []
33	    }
34	
35	    public func encode(to encoder: Encoder) throws {
36	        var container = encoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
37	        try container.encode(usages, forKey: .usages)
38	        // Upstream convention: absent key when no ongoing outage — mirror that on write
39	        // so the file never carries a stale empty array between incidents.
40	        if !outages.isEmpty {
41	            try container.encode(outages, forKey: .outages)
42	        }
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	// MARK: - VendorUsageEntry
47	
48	public struct VendorUsageEntry: Codable, Equatable, Sendable, Identifiable {
49	    public var id: String { "\(vendor.rawValue):\(account.rawValue)" }
50	
51	    public let vendor: Vendor
52	    public let account: AccountEmail
53	    public var isActive: Bool
54	    public var lastAcquiredOn: ISODate?
55	    public var lastError: UsageError?
56	    public var metrics: [UsageMetric]
57	
58	    public init(
59	        vendor: Vendor,
60	        account: AccountEmail,
61	        isActive: Bool = false,
62	        lastAcquiredOn: ISODate? = nil,
63	        lastError: UsageError? = nil,
64	        metrics: [UsageMetric] = []
65	    ) {
66	        self.vendor = vendor
67	        self.account = account
68	        self.isActive = isActive
69	        self.lastAcquiredOn = lastAcquiredOn
70	        self.lastError = lastError
71	        self.metrics = metrics
72	    }
73	}
74	
75	// MARK: - Error
76	
77	public struct UsageError: Codable, Equatable, Sendable {
78	    public let timestamp: ISODate
79	    public let type: String
80	
81	    public init(timestamp: ISODate, type: String) {
82	        self.timestamp = timestamp
83	        self.type = type
84	    }
85	}
86	
87	// MARK: - Outage
88	
89	/// An active incident on a vendor's platform, written by an upstream status-fetching process.
90	/// The upstream writer removes entries as incidents resolve, so a missing or empty
91	/// `outages` array means "all clear".
92	public struct Outage: Codable, Equatable, Sendable, Identifiable {
93	    public let vendor: Vendor
94	    public let errorMessage: String
95	    public let severity: OutageSeverity
96	    public let since: ISODate
97	    public let href: URL?
98	
99	    /// Composite id — outages have no stable upstream id in this shape, so we
100	    /// derive one from the fields that together identify a single incident row.
101	    public var id: String {
102	        "\(vendor.rawValue)|\(since.rawValue)|\(href?.absoluteString ?? "")"
103	    }
104	
105	    public init(
106	        vendor: Vendor,
107	        errorMessage: String,
108	        severity: OutageSeverity,
109	        since: ISODate,
110	        href: URL? = nil
111	    ) {
112	        self.vendor = vendor
113	        self.errorMessage = errorMessage
114	        self.severity = severity
115	        self.since = since
116	        self.href = href
117	    }
118	
119	    private enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
120	        case vendor, errorMessage, severity, since, href
121	    }
122	
123	    public init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
124	        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
125	        vendor = try container.decode(Vendor.self, forKey: .vendor)
126	        errorMessage = try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .errorMessage)
127	        severity = try container.decode(OutageSeverity.self, forKey: .severity)
128	        since = try container.decode(ISODate.self, forKey: .since)
129	        if let hrefString = try container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .href) {
130	            guard let parsed = URL(string: hrefString) else {
131	                throw DecodingError.dataCorruptedError(
132	                    forKey: .href, in: container,
133	                    debugDescription: "Invalid URL string: \(hrefString)")
134	            }
135	            href = parsed
136	        } else {
137	            href = nil
138	        }
139	    }
140	}

142	// MARK: - Metric (polymorphic on "type" discriminator)
143	
144	public enum UsageMetric: Codable, Equatable, Sendable {
145	    case timeWindow(name: String, resetAt: ISODate?, windowDuration: DurationMinutes, usagePercent: UsagePercent)
146	    case payAsYouGo(name: String, currentAmount: Double, currency: String)
147	    /// A metric type not yet known to this client; retained for round-trip fidelity.
148	    case unknown(String)
149	
150	    // MARK: Kind
151	
152	    public var kind: MetricKind {
153	        switch self {
154	        case .timeWindow:       .timeWindow
155	        case .payAsYouGo:       .payAsYouGo
156	        case .unknown(let t):   .unknown(t)
157	        }
158	    }
159	
160	    // MARK: Coding
161	
162	    private enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
163	        case type, name, resetAt
164	        case windowDuration = "windowDurationMinutes"
165	        case usagePercent, currentAmount, currency
166	    }
167	
168	    public init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
169	        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
170	        let metricKind = try container.decode(MetricKind.self, forKey: .type)
171	        switch metricKind {
172	        case .timeWindow:
173	            self = .timeWindow(
174	                name: try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .name),
175	                resetAt: try container.decodeIfPresent(ISODate.self, forKey: .resetAt),
176	                windowDuration: try container.decode(DurationMinutes.self, forKey: .windowDuration),
177	                usagePercent: try container.decode(UsagePercent.self, forKey: .usagePercent)
178	            )
179	        case .payAsYouGo:
180	            self = .payAsYouGo(
181	                name: try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .name),
182	                currentAmount: try container.decode(Double.self, forKey: .currentAmount),
183	                currency: try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .currency)
184	            )
185	        case .unknown(let t):
186	            self = .unknown(t)
187	        }
188	    }
189	
190	    public func encode(to encoder: Encoder) throws {
191	        var container = encoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
192	        switch self {
193	        case let .timeWindow(name, resetAt, windowDuration, usagePercent):
194	            // Connector contract: a present `resetAt` must be a strict ISO 8601
195	            // datetime. Enforced as a DEBUG assertion so that any new connector
196	            // emitting a date-only or otherwise unparseable string fails its
197	            // tests immediately — production stays permissive so that legacy
198	            // payloads round-trip without crashing the app.
199	            if let resetAt {
200	                assert(
201	                    resetAt.isStrictlyValid,
202	                    "UsageMetric.timeWindow resetAt must be a strict ISO 8601 datetime, got: '\(resetAt.rawValue)'. Use ISODate.parsing(_:) or parsingFlexibleDate(_:) at the connector boundary."
203	                )
204	            }
205	            try container.encode(MetricKind.timeWindow, forKey: .type)
206	            try container.encode(name, forKey: .name)
207	            try container.encodeIfPresent(resetAt, forKey: .resetAt)
208	            try container.encode(windowDuration, forKey: .windowDuration)
209	            try container.encode(usagePercent, forKey: .usagePercent)
210	        case let .payAsYouGo(name, currentAmount, currency):
211	            try container.encode(MetricKind.payAsYouGo, forKey: .type)
212	            try container.encode(name, forKey: .name)
213	            try container.encode(currentAmount, forKey: .currentAmount)
214	            try container.encode(currency, forKey: .currency)
215	        case let .unknown(t):
216	            // Only the type discriminator round-trips; unknown fields are not preserved
217	            try container.encode(MetricKind.unknown(t), forKey: .type)
218	        }
219	    }
220	}

content.thinking:
I'm going to take a practical approach here: reuse the incidents JSON proxy to extract component IDs from the affected components field since the connector already parses that data, and I'll add an `id` field to the `IncidentIOAffectedComponent` model for filtering. Then I'll fetch components from a separate endpoint that incident.io status pages typically expose.

The roadmap says RSC parsing is required for component discovery since there's no public JSON endpoint, so I need to build a parser that can extract the component objects from the Next.js RSC payload embedded in the HTML. The payload comes as stringified chunks in script tags, so I'll fetch the HTML, extract those chunks, and use regex to find objects matching the component shape with `id`, `name`, and `group_id` fields.

Building a regex-based parser in Swift to extract these component objects from the RSC stream...

For storage, I'll set up a cache file that tracks the last refresh timestamp and stores the cached components, with a subscription store in `AppPreferences` to track which components the user has subscribed to.

Now I'm ready to start implementing. I'll need to create a few key pieces: the data models for status components, a file manager to handle the cache persistence, a parser for extracting component data from the RSC stream, and a registry service that coordinates everything. I'll also update the preferences system to track subscriptions and modify the existing connector to filter by subscribed components and include the component metadata in the response.

Let me begin writing the implementation...

1	import Foundation
2	
3	/// One-shot migration converting the legacy global "outage warning" preference
4	/// (`menuBarOutageWarningEnabled` + `menuBarOutageWarningText`) into per-segment
5	/// `showOutageWarning` / `outageWarningText` fields on `MenuBarSegmentConfig`.
6	///
7	/// Behaviour:
8	/// * If the user never explicitly set the flag, the legacy default was on, so we
9	///   activate the warning on every currently-configured segment using the stored
10	///   (or default) warning text.
11	/// * If the user explicitly disabled the flag, segments are left untouched.
12	/// * If the user explicitly enabled it, segments inherit the stored warning text.
13	/// * Once migrated, the legacy UserDefaults keys are removed and the migration
14	///   flag is set so subsequent launches no-op (preserving the user's per-segment
15	///   toggles).
16	@MainActor
17	public enum OutageWarningMigration {
18	    private static let legacyEnabledKey = "ai-tracker.menuBarOutageWarningEnabled"
19	    private static let legacyEnabledInitializedKey = "ai-tracker.menuBarOutageWarningEnabledInitialized"
20	    private static let legacyTextKey = "ai-tracker.menuBarOutageWarningText"
21	    private static let migrationCompletedKey = "ai-tracker.menuBarOutageWarningMigrationCompleted"
22	
23	    public static func migrateIfNeeded(
24	        preferences: any AppPreferences,
25	        defaults: UserDefaults = .standard
26	    ) {
27	        guard !defaults.bool(forKey: migrationCompletedKey) else { return }
28	
29	        let initialized = defaults.bool(forKey: legacyEnabledInitializedKey)
30	        let wasEnabled: Bool
31	        if initialized {
32	            wasEnabled = defaults.bool(forKey: legacyEnabledKey)
33	        } else {
34	            // Legacy default was on (opt-out); treat un-touched as enabled.
35	            wasEnabled = true
36	        }
37	
38	        if wasEnabled {
39	            let storedText = defaults.string(forKey: legacyTextKey)
40	            let text = (storedText?.isEmpty == false)
41	                ? storedText!
42	                : MenuBarSegmentConfig.defaultOutageWarningText
43	            let segments = preferences.menuBarSegments
44	            if !segments.isEmpty {
45	                preferences.menuBarSegments = segments.map { config in
46	                    var copy = config
47	                    copy.showOutageWarning = true
48	                    copy.outageWarningText = text
49	                    return copy
50	                }
51	            }
52	        }
53	
54	        defaults.removeObject(forKey: legacyEnabledKey)
55	        defaults.removeObject(forKey: legacyEnabledInitializedKey)
56	        defaults.removeObject(forKey: legacyTextKey)
57	        defaults.set(true, forKey: migrationCompletedKey)
58	    }
59	}

# SwiftLint configuration for AIUsagesTrackers.
#
# Rule codes mirror .claude/rules/swift-quality.md — keep the mapping in sync.
# Severities: `error` fails the build; `warning` surfaces in logs / IDE.

included:
  - Sources
  - Tests

# Narrow the SwiftLint surface to exactly the codes documented in
# .claude/rules/swift-quality.md. Built-in rules are deliberately NOT enabled —
# we want a low-noise, high-signal lint focused on the patterns that reviews
# keep flagging. Reach for built-in rules only if a review finding recurs that
# a custom rule cannot express precisely.
only_rules:
  - custom_rules

custom_rules:
  e1_try_silencing_critical_fs:
    name: "try? on critical filesystem op"
    regex: 'try\?\s+(FileManager\.default\.)?(createDirectory|moveItem|copyItem|setAttributes)\b'
    message: "try? swallows failure on a correctness-critical FS op — use do/catch (SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING §1)"
    severity: error

  e2_flock_lock_ex_without_nb:
    name: "flock LOCK_EX without LOCK_NB"
    regex: 'flock\([^)]*LOCK_EX(?![^)]*LOCK_NB)'
    message: "flock with LOCK_EX must be OR'd with LOCK_NB and wrapped in a timeout loop (SWIFT-CONCURRENCY / SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS)"
    severity: error
    excluded:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/.*\\.swift"

  w1_nonisolated_unsafe:
    name: "nonisolated(unsafe) in production"
    regex: 'nonisolated\(unsafe\)'
    excluded_match_kinds:
      - comment
      - doccomment
    message: "nonisolated(unsafe) bypasses Swift isolation — prefer actor-isolated storage or per-call allocation (SWIFT-CONCURRENCY)"
    severity: warning
    excluded:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/.*\\.swift"

  w3_task_sleep_literal_in_tests:
    name: "Task.sleep with fixed literal in tests"
    regex: 'Task\.sleep\([^)]*[0-9]'
    message: "Task.sleep with a fixed literal is fragile under CI load — poll the observable state with the eventually() helper (SWIFT-TESTABILITY)"
    severity: warning
    included:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/.*\\.swift"

  w4_unchecked_sendable:
    name: "@unchecked Sendable"
    regex: '@unchecked\s+Sendable'
    excluded_match_kinds:
      - comment
      - doccomment
    message: "@unchecked Sendable requires an adjacent justification comment describing the single-threaded contract (SWIFT-CONCURRENCY)"
    severity: warning

  w5_duration_literal_in_comment:
    name: "Duration literal embedded in comment"
    regex: '\b\d+\s*(hours?|days?|minutes?|seconds?|ms)\b'
    match_kinds:
      - comment
    message: "Comment embeds a bare `N unit` duration — lift to a named constant whose declaration site carries the rationale (SWIFT-TESTABILITY §Comment WHY)"
    severity: warning

  e3_credential_locator_writes:
    name: "credential locator writes"
    regex: 'SecItemAdd|SecItemUpdate|SecItemDelete|SecKeychainItemCreate|SecKeychainItemModifyAttributesAndData'
    excluded_match_kinds:
      - comment
      - doccomment
    included:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/.*CredentialLocator\\.swift"
    excluded:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Connectors/CredentialLocator\\.swift"
    message: "Credential locators are read-only by contract — see VENDOR-PLUGIN-CONTRACT.md §5"
    severity: error

  w6_payload_log_not_through_proxy:
    name: "payload log not routed through LoggingProxy"
    regex: 'logger\.log\([^)]*(payload|response|request body|API\s+payload)'
    excluded_match_kinds:
      - comment
      - doccomment
    included:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Connectors/.*Connector\\.swift"
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Connectors/.*CredentialLocator\\.swift"
    excluded:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Connectors/UsageConnector\\.swift"
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Connectors/StatusConnector\\.swift"
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Connectors/CredentialLocator\\.swift"
    message: "Payload-bearing log lines must go through LoggingProxy (VENDOR-PLUGIN-CONTRACT.md §6)"
    severity: warning

import Foundation

/// Centralised UserDefaults key names, prefixed to avoid collisions with
/// other subsystems that might share the same defaults suite.
public enum AppPreferenceKeys: String {
    case refreshIntervalSeconds = "ai-tracker.refreshIntervalSeconds"
    case launchAtLogin = "ai-tracker.launchAtLogin"
    case logLevel = "ai-tracker.logLevel"
    case menuBarSegments = "ai-tracker.menuBarSegments"
    case menuBarSegmentsInitialized = "ai-tracker.menuBarSegmentsInitialized"
    case menuBarSeparator = "ai-tracker.menuBarSeparator"
    case chartConfigurations = "ai-tracker.chartConfigurations"
    case chartConfigurationsInitialized = "ai-tracker.chartConfigurationsInitialized"
    case ignoredAccounts = "ai-tracker.ignoredAccounts"
    case updatesAutoCheckEnabled = "ai-tracker.updatesAutoCheckEnabled"
    case updatesAutoCheckEnabledInitialized = "ai-tracker.updatesAutoCheckEnabledInitialized"

content.subject: Explore architecture (preferences, plugins, settings UI, persistence)

content.subject: Design StatusPlatform model + component cache (data types, file)
content.description: Create value objects for StatusPlatform, StatusComponent (id, name, groupId), cache schema indexed by (platform, status_page_host, group_root_id), persistence helper.

content.subject: Implement incident.io RSC parser for OpenAI status page
content.description: Parse Next.js RSC payload embedded in https://status.openai.com/ HTML, extract {component_id, name, group_id} triples, filter to Codex group root. Fail loud on schema change.

content.subject: Add subscription preferences (per-platform/component) with default ON
content.description: Extend AppPreferences with subscription set keyed by component_id, default ON for unknown discovered components. Store last refresh timestamp.

content.subject: Filter CodexStatusConnector.fetchOutages by subscribed components
content.description: Inject subscription provider. Extract affected_components.component_id; drop incident if zero overlap with subscriptions.

content.subject: Add Status preferences tab in SettingsView
content.description: List cached Codex components with name primary + component_id mono-small secondary, toggle per component, last refresh timestamp, Refresh now button (synchronous refresh).

content.subject: Wire 24h lazy refresh on app start
content.description: On AppDelegate startup, if cache older than 24h or missing, fire refresh asynchronously. Otherwise no work.

content.subject: Tests: parser (golden + tampered), filter, prefs, cache
content.description: Unit tests covering RSC parser happy path + parse failure, filter retention/drop, preferences default-ON for new components, atomic cache I/O.

content.subject: Update docs/vendors/codex.md + Change log entry

content.thinking:
I'm debugging a regex issue with the parser test — the pattern for matching JSON payloads with whitespace isn't working because it's excluding braces when it shouldn't need to. I'm also second-guessing my ULID regex validation, specifically whether I have the right Crockford base32 character set. The test is failing because the regex expects `"id":"..."` with no spaces, but the pretty-printed JSON payload has `"id": "..."` with a space after the colon. I should make the regex flexible to handle optional whitespace around colons and between fields, or better yet, parse the unescaped payload more robustly by finding each field independently within a surrounding window rather than trying to match the entire pattern at once.

Now I'm realizing there's a deeper inconsistency in how I'm handling the nil resolver. My StatusComponentRegistry docstring says nil should drop everything, but the CodexStatusConnector treats nil as "no filter, pass through all incidents." The legacy init also uses a nil resolver, so if I change nil to mean drop-all, it'll break existing tests. I need to distinguish between "no filter applied" (the legacy behavior) and "filter enabled but subscriptions are empty" (which should drop incidents).

The cleaner approach is to have the resolver return `Set<StatusComponentID>?` where nil means no filtering at all, an empty Set means filter is active but nothing's subscribed, and a non-empty Set means only those components pass through. This way the legacy init can keep returning nil for backward compatibility, while the production resolver returns an empty Set on fresh installs to enforce the roadmap requirement of showing an empty Status tab until parsing succeeds.

I need to update my test expectations to reflect this distinction—testing the empty Set behavior instead of expecting nil to drop everything—and then make the parser regex more tolerant of whitespace.

content.thinking:
The filter tests are passing, but I'm hitting issues with two parser tests. The first one, "supports both field orderings," is failing because it can't locate "CLI" in the expected object, and I'm noticing the ULID `01BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB` looks suspicious—B characters shouldn't be valid in ULIDs.

Actually, wait—B is in the A-H range, so that's fine. The real problem is my windowing approach: when I search for the `name` field within a 400-character window around each ID, I'm grabbing the first match in that window, which means for the second ID I might be pulling the first ID's name instead. The second failing test, "extracts Codex child components from a plain JSON payload," is producing 3 results instead of 2 because "Audio" is now matching the regex pattern even though it has a different group_id, and the window overlap is causing the parser to assign wrong names to components. I need to abandon the windowing strategy and instead extract individual JSON object boundaries first before searching for fields.

Let me try anchoring on the ID and finding the closest `name` and `group_id` fields within a tighter distance, or better yet, scan for all three field types and group them by proximity—whichever set has the smallest gaps wins. Since the payload is typically compact JSON without whitespace, I could also try finding candidate object substrings by looking for `{...}` boundaries and parsing each one.

Actually, the cleanest approach: for each `"id":"<ULID>"` match, find the nearest `{` before it and `}` after it (stopping before the next ID), extract that substring, and parse it as JSON. The incident.io component objects appear to be flat with no nesting, so this should work reliably.

Actually, the cleanest approach is to just assign each name and group_id to whichever ID is closest to it, regardless of position. Then for each ID, I collect all the names and group_ids that were assigned to it.

content.thinking:
I'm looking at a failing test for HTML unescaping where the escaped quotes in the JSON payload need to be converted back to regular quotes, but the unescaping process appears to be cutting off mid-result.

The real issue is that my brace-walking logic enters string mode at the first quote after `[1,` and then gets confused about when it's inside or outside a string literal. Since the entire JSON object is wrapped in quotes as part of the RSC payload, the walker needs to properly track string boundaries to identify where the actual braces are — but it's treating the opening brace of the JSON object as if it's still inside the string, which breaks the slice logic.

The simplest fix is to stop tracking strings altogether in the brace walker. In practice, the JSON values we're extracting (names, ULIDs) don't contain braces, so a naive brace counter should work fine without worrying about string content. This avoids the whole problem of trying to distinguish between the outer JavaScript string layer and the inner JSON structure after unescaping.

# Codex (ChatGPT)

<!--
  Read `docs/VENDOR-PLUGIN-CONTRACT.md` first; it defines the shape of
  this document. If this file disagrees with the contract, the contract
  wins.
-->

## Endpoints

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Method | URL | Headers | Timeout | Response Content-Type |
|--------|-----|---------|---------|-----------------------|
| GET    | `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage` | `Authorization: Bearer <oauth>`, `ChatGPT-Account-Id: <account_id>`, `User-Agent: OpenUsage` | 5s | `application/json` |

The `User-Agent: OpenUsage` value reproduces the header used by the
upstream `openusage` reverse-engineering effort and avoids bot filtering.

## Credential sources

_verified: 2026-05-07_

Cascade (read-only — the app never writes any of these):

1. `$CODEX_HOME/auth.json` if `CODEX_HOME` is set.
2. `~/.config/codex/auth.json`
3. `~/.codex/auth.json`
4. **macOS Keychain entry** `Codex Auth` (fallback when no auth file
   exists).

The `codex` CLI owns lifecycle for all sources. The locator never calls
`SecItemAdd`, never invokes `codex auth`, never writes any of these
files.

`auth.json` shape (only the fields the connector reads):

```jsonc
{
  "tokens": {
    "access_token": "<jwt>",
    "account_id": "user-...",
    "id_token": "<jwt>"
  },
  "last_refresh": "2026-05-07T00:00:00.000000Z"
}
```

The active account email is derived from JWT claims (`email` or
`https://api.openai.com/profile.email`) on `tokens.id_token` first, then
`tokens.access_token`. The `email` field also surfaces in the API
response itself and overrides the JWT-derived value at fetch time.

## Sanitized fields

_verified: 2026-05-07_

Drives the leakage test in `Tests/Fixtures/codex-full-payload.json`.

| Location | Field path | Redaction style |
|----------|-----------|-----------------|
| Header   | `Authorization` value | full removal (`<redacted>`) |
| Header   | `ChatGPT-Account-Id` value | full removal (account identifier the vendor treats as private) |
| Body     | any `*token*` / `*key*` / `*secret*` / `*password*` / `*credential*` key | full removal |
| Body     | any `*email*` key | full removal |
| Body     | `user_id`, `account_id` | full removal (account identifiers) |
| Message  | `[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+` matches | replace with `<email>` |

`user_id` and `account_id` look indistinguishable from each other in the
captured payload (same value); both are treated as private.

## Plan variants observed

_verified: 2026-05-07_

### Plus plan (verified by tester)

```jsonc
<!-- captured: 2026-05-07, plan: Plus, login: redacted -->
{
  "user_id": "<redacted>",
  "account_id": "<redacted>",
  "email": "<email>",
  "plan_type": "plus",
  "rate_limit": {
    "allowed": true,
    "limit_reached": false,
    "primary_window": {
      "used_percent": 30,
      "limit_window_seconds": 18000,
      "reset_after_seconds": 2473,
      "reset_at": 1778114717
    },
    "secondary_window": {
      "used_percent": 39,
      "limit_window_seconds": 604800,
      "reset_after_seconds": 1690,
      "reset_at": 1778113934
    }
  },
  "code_review_rate_limit": null,
  "additional_rate_limits": null,
  "credits": {
    "has_credits": false,
    "unlimited": false,
    "overage_limit_reached": false,
    "balance": "0",
    "approx_local_messages": [0, 0],
    "approx_cloud_messages": [0, 0]
  },
  "spend_control": { "reached": false, "individual_limit": null },
  "rate_limit_reached_type": null,
  "promo": null,
  "referral_beacon": null
}
```

### Pro plan (assumed, not yet verified by tester)

Same shape as Plus. Tighter `limit_window_seconds` on `primary_window`
likely; `code_review_rate_limit` may be populated.

### Spark / Team / Enterprise plans (assumed, not yet verified by tester)

`additional_rate_limits` block expected to be populated with per-model
quotas: `[{"limit_name": "<model>", "rate_limit": {"primary_window": ...,
"secondary_window": ...}}]`. The connector emits a metric per
`limit_name × window`.

### Pay-as-you-go credits (assumed, not yet verified by tester)

`credits.has_credits: true` with `balance` as a numeric remaining
allowance. Connector subtracts from a hard-coded pool size
(`creditPoolTotal = 1000`) to emit a `.payAsYouGo("Credits used", ...)`
metric. Header fallback `x-codex-credits-balance` is consumed when the
body block is absent.

## Metric semantics

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Swift metric | Source field | Reset cadence | Unit | Edge cases |
|--------------|--------------|---------------|------|------------|
| `.timeWindow("Session (5h)")` | `rate_limit.primary_window.used_percent` / `reset_at` | rolling, duration from `limit_window_seconds` (fallback 300 minutes) | percent | missing `used_percent` → metric omitted |
| `.timeWindow("Weekly (7d)")` | `rate_limit.secondary_window.*` | rolling, duration from `limit_window_seconds` (fallback 10080 minutes) | percent | same |
| `.timeWindow("Code Review (7d)")` | `code_review_rate_limit.primary_window.*` | weekly | percent | absent block → metric omitted |
| `.timeWindow("<limit_name> (5h)")` | `additional_rate_limits[i].rate_limit.primary_window.*` | rolling | percent | one per `limit_name` |
| `.timeWindow("<limit_name> Weekly (7d)")` | `additional_rate_limits[i].rate_limit.secondary_window.*` | weekly | percent | one per `limit_name` |
| `.payAsYouGo("Credits used", ..., currency: "credits")` | `credits.balance` (body) → `x-codex-credits-balance` (header) | n/a | credits | `has_credits: false` → metric omitted; balance clamped to ≥ 0 |

Window durations are returned in seconds via `limit_window_seconds`; the
connector divides by 60 and falls back to fixed values when the field is
absent or zero.

## Time semantics

_verified: 2026-05-07_

- Reset values arrive as **Unix epoch seconds**: `"reset_at": 1778114717`.
- Connector wraps with `Date(timeIntervalSince1970:)` and emits as ISO
  8601 via `ISODate(date:)`.

## Error catalog

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Status | Connector response |
|--------|--------------------|
| 200    | parse, emit metrics, refresh `lastKnownMetrics`; if no known window block, surface `parse_error` |
| 401    | surface `token_expired`; no metric refresh |
| 429    | preserve last-known metrics, mark active, surface `http_429` |
| other  | surface `http_<code>`; do not refresh metrics |

`identity_unresolved` is logged (no entry written) when both the
response email and the cached email are missing — exceptional state where
the active account cannot be attributed.

## Status page

_verified: 2026-05-12_

| URL | Format | Component filter |
|-----|--------|-----------------|
| `https://status.openai.com/proxy/status.openai.com/incidents` | incident.io public-page proxy (JSON) | none — all incidents on this page are OpenAI-specific |

OpenAI moved off statuspage.io to incident.io; the legacy
`status.openai.com/api/v2/incidents/unresolved.json` path now returns
HTTP 404. The page itself fetches the JSON document above (it lists
every incident, resolved + active).

`CodexStatusConnector` retains incidents whose `status` is anything
other than `"resolved"` (i.e. `investigating`, `identified`,
`monitoring`, scheduled or in-progress maintenance). Severity is
derived from the worst entry in `affected_components[].status`:

| incident.io component status | `OutageSeverity` |
|------------------------------|------------------|
| `full_outage`, `major_outage` | `critical`       |
| `partial_outage`              | `major`          |
| `degraded_performance`        | `minor`          |
| `under_maintenance`           | `maintenance`    |

Maintenance windows (`type: "maintenance"` at the incident level) are
mapped to `maintenance` regardless of component status. The clickable
href is built from the incident `id` against
`https://status.openai.com/incidents/<id>`.

Schema reference of an incident entry (fields the connector consumes):

```jsonc
{
  "id": "01KRBZTV511HR7SWVFZJ0438D6",
  "name": "Elevated error rates with GPT 5.5",
  "status": "resolved",                 // "investigating" | "identified" | "monitoring" | "resolved" | scheduled / in-progress for maintenance
  "type": "incident",                   // or "maintenance"
  "published_at": "2026-05-11T16:11:00Z",
  "affected_components": [
    { "status": "degraded_performance", "current_status": "operational" }
  ]
}
```

## Known unknowns

- Pro plan response shape on a Pro-only account.
- Enterprise / Team plan shapes — `additional_rate_limits` content
  unverified.
- Live `credits.has_credits: true` payload with non-zero balance.
- `code_review_rate_limit` populated payload (any plan).
- Headers Codex returns on 429 (Retry-After cadence).

## Source references

- `openusage` upstream project — initial reverse engineering of the
  `wham/usage` endpoint (https://github.com/openusage/openusage) (retrieved
  2026-05-07).
- Maintainer's own captures during development of `CodexConnector`.

## Change log

- 2026-05-07 — initial capture: Plus plan response shape, Unix epoch
  reset semantics, `User-Agent: OpenUsage` header. Cascade order
  `$CODEX_HOME` → `~/.config/codex` → `~/.codex` → keychain.

# Codex (ChatGPT)

> **Last verified:** 2026-05-13 by @fcamblor on ChatGPT Plus plan

<!--
  Read `docs/VENDOR-PLUGIN-CONTRACT.md` first; it defines the shape of
  this document. If this file disagrees with the contract, the contract
  wins.
-->

## Endpoints

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Method | URL | Headers | Timeout | Response Content-Type |
|--------|-----|---------|---------|-----------------------|
| GET    | `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage` | `Authorization: Bearer <oauth>`, `ChatGPT-Account-Id: <account_id>`, `User-Agent: OpenUsage` | 5s | `application/json` |

The `User-Agent: OpenUsage` value reproduces the header used by the
upstream `openusage` reverse-engineering effort and avoids bot filtering.

## Credential sources

_verified: 2026-05-07_

Cascade (read-only — the app never writes any of these):

1. `$CODEX_HOME/auth.json` if `CODEX_HOME` is set.
2. `~/.config/codex/auth.json`
3. `~/.codex/auth.json`
4. **macOS Keychain entry** `Codex Auth` (fallback when no auth file
   exists).

The `codex` CLI owns lifecycle for all sources. The locator never calls
`SecItemAdd`, never invokes `codex auth`, never writes any of these
files.

`auth.json` shape (only the fields the connector reads):

```jsonc
{
  "tokens": {
    "access_token": "<jwt>",
    "account_id": "user-...",
    "id_token": "<jwt>"
  },
  "last_refresh": "2026-05-07T00:00:00.000000Z"
}
```

The active account email is derived from JWT claims (`email` or
`https://api.openai.com/profile.email`) on `tokens.id_token` first, then
`tokens.access_token`. The `email` field also surfaces in the API
response itself and overrides the JWT-derived value at fetch time.

## Sanitized fields

_verified: 2026-05-07_

Drives the leakage test in `Tests/Fixtures/codex-full-payload.json`.

| Location | Field path | Redaction style |
|----------|-----------|-----------------|
| Header   | `Authorization` value | full removal (`<redacted>`) |
| Header   | `ChatGPT-Account-Id` value | full removal (account identifier the vendor treats as private) |
| Body     | any `*token*` / `*key*` / `*secret*` / `*password*` / `*credential*` key | full removal |
| Body     | any `*email*` key | full removal |
| Body     | `user_id`, `account_id` | full removal (account identifiers) |
| Message  | `[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+` matches | replace with `<email>` |

`user_id` and `account_id` look indistinguishable from each other in the
captured payload (same value); both are treated as private.

```jsonc
      "reset_at": 1778114717
    },

      "used_percent": 39,

      "reset_after_seconds": 1690,
      "reset_at": 1778113934
    }
  },

  "credits": {
    "has_credits": false,
    "unlimited": false,
    "overage_limit_reached": false,
    "balance": "0",
    "approx_local_messages": [0, 0],
    "approx_cloud_messages": [0, 0]
  },
  "spend_control": { "reached": false, "individual_limit": null },
  "rate_limit_reached_type": null,
  "promo": null,
  "referral_beacon": null
}
```

### Pro plan (assumed, not yet verified by tester)

Schema reference of an incident entry (fields the connector consumes):

```jsonc
{
  "id": "01KRBZTV511HR7SWVFZJ0438D6",
  "name": "Elevated error rates with GPT 5.5",
  "status": "resolved",                 // "investigating" | "identified" | "monitoring" | "resolved" | scheduled / in-progress for maintenance

```

### Codex component group

incident.io exposes no public unauthenticated JSON endpoint for the
component hierarchy, so the discovery layer parses the Next.js RSC
payload embedded in `https://status.openai.com/` and extracts
`{component_id, name, group_id}` triples whose `group_id` matches the
Codex group root:

| Group root id | Observed children (subject to change as OpenAI ships products) |
|---|---|
| `01KMKF9EBTCD8BN9PG8DJZXRSQ` | Codex Web, App, Codex API, CLI, VS Code extension |

The cache is refreshed lazily on app start when older than 24h, plus
on-demand via the "Refresh now" button in the Status preferences tab.
If parsing fails the connector keeps the last successful cache; on a
fresh install with no successful refresh yet, the Codex outage banner
stays silent until the parser succeeds.
